studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Measurements were collected at baseline, 3 months, 6 months, 9 months, and 12 months post-baseline.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received immediate 12-month access to the web-based program Optimune in addition to care as usual (CAU). The control group received CAU and access to Optimune after a 6-month delay (waitlist control).
studies.collected_data: Primary outcomes: Plasma concentrations of CRP, IL-6, TNF-α, stimulated IL-6, and stimulated TNF-α. Secondary outcomes: Plasma concentrations of various cytokines, cortisol awakening response (CAR), cancer-related fatigue (BFI-9), emotional stress (IES-R), depression (PHQ-9), anxiety (GAD-7), fear of progression (PA-F12), usefulness of the program, and negative effects (INEP). Other outcomes: Recurrence of breast cancer, frequency of common cold or virus flu, frequency of unscheduled medical encounters.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Measurements were collected at pre-treatment (T0), 3 months (T1), and 6 months (T2). The intervention group had access to the program for 12 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received immediate 12-month access to the web-based Optimune program in addition to care as usual (CAU). The control group received CAU and access to Optimune after a 3-month delay (waitlist control).
studies.collected_data: Primary outcomes: Quality of Life (WHOQOL-BREF), Physical Activity (IPAQ-short), Diet Quality (FQQ). Secondary outcomes: Cancer-related fatigue (BFI-9), Cancer-related emotional stress (IES-R), Depression (PHQ-9), Anxiety (GAD-7), Fear of progression (PA-F12), Insomnia (ISI), Usefulness of the program (questionnaire), Negative Effects (INEP). Other outcomes: Recurrence of breast cancer, Frequency of common cold or virus flu, Frequency of unscheduled medical encounters, Body Mass Index, Psychotherapy (all determined by questionnaires).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Follow-up surveys are still ongoing despite the study being discontinued.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: access to the online program optimune for 365 days in addition to routine treatment (care as usual). Control group: routine treatment (care as usual), access to the online program optimune for 365 days after completion of the study.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Quality of life (total score Assessment of Quality of Life; AQoL-8D) at T1 (3 months after baseline). Secondary Outcomes: General distress (Depression-Anxiety-Stress-Scales, DASS-21), Work and social ability (Work and Social Adjustment Scale, WSAS), Fatigue (Brief Fatigue Inventory, BFI), responder rate of AQoL-8D, subscales and dimensions of AQoL-8D.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary endpoints were assessed at three months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received care as usual (CAU) plus 12-month access to Optimune immediately after randomization. Control group received CAU and Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.collected_data: Quality of life (QoL), physical activity, dietary habits.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Significant effects on QoL (d = 0.27, 95% CI: 0.07-0.48) and dietary habits (d = 0.36, 95% CI: 0.15-0.56). Effect on physical exercise was not significant (d = 0.30; 95% CI: 0.10-0.51).

audit.study_title: Evaluating a Novel Web-based Intervention for Breast Cancer Survivors
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Evaluating the Effectiveness of an Unguided Digital Therapeutic for Breast Cancer Survivors – optimune – Randomized Controlled Trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Results of the Optimune trial: A randomized controlled trial evaluating a novel Internet intervention for breast cancer survivors.
